behaviorsAudited.behavior: message-adapter.ts builds a streamingToolUses: StreamingToolUse[] from the same input event stream it consumes today, using the upstream contract from messages.ts handleMessageFromStream (content_block_start + input_json_delta cases at messages.ts:3029, 3062-3079)
On content_block_start whose contentBlock.type === 'tool_use', append {index, contentBlock, unparsedToolInput: ''} to the array

On input_json_delta with a known index, replace the matching element with {...element, unparsedToolInput: element.unparsedToolInput + delta} (immutable update, matches upstream return shape at messages.ts:3071-3077)
On input_json_delta with an index that does not match any existing element, return the array unchanged (matches messages.ts:3068-3070 early return when find() yields no element)
On stream completion / new turn boundary AND on turn_aborted, the array is cleared (matches REPL.tsx:1609 and 2940 setStreamingToolUses([]) calls)
behaviorsAudited.implementedAt: runtime/src/tui/openclaude/message-adapter.ts:586-597 (turn_started/turn_start case sets streamingToolUses.length = 0) AND runtime/src/tui/openclaude/message-adapter.ts:613-622 (turn_aborted case sets streamingToolUses.length = 0)

behaviorsAudited.behavior: use-session-transcript.ts exposes streamingToolUses on the transcript snapshot shape consumed by App.tsx
StreamingToolUse type is imported from runtime/src/agenc/upstream/utils/messages.ts so the AgenC bridge type matches the upstream <Messages> consumer prop type exactly
behaviorsAudited.assertedAt: message-adapter.streaming-tool-use.parity.test.ts:4 (test file imports the same type from the same path)

edgeCasesAudited.edgeCase: Empty input event sequence yields streamingToolUses=[]
Single tool with a single delta yields one element with the delta concatenated into unparsedToolInput
edgeCasesAudited.assertedAt: message-adapter.streaming-tool-use.parity.test.ts:E5.2 'single tool block_start + single delta yields one element with the delta concatenated into unparsedToolInput'

edgeCasesAudited.edgeCase: Two concurrent tools at indices 0 and 1 with interleaved deltas yield two elements each containing only their own deltas
edgeCasesAudited.assertedAt: message-adapter.streaming-tool-use.parity.test.ts:E5.3 'two concurrent tools at indices 0 and 1 with interleaved deltas accumulate independently'

edgeCasesAudited.edgeCase: Out-of-order delta (delta arrives before content_block_start for that index) does not throw and does not create a phantom element — array unchanged
edgeCasesAudited.assertedAt: message-adapter.streaming-tool-use.parity.test.ts:E5.4 'out-of-order delta ... does not throw and does not create a phantom element'

edgeCasesAudited.edgeCase: Delta with empty partial_json string is appended as the empty string (no-op concat, no exception)
edgeCasesAudited.assertedAt: message-adapter.streaming-tool-use.parity.test.ts:E5.5 'delta with empty partial_json string is appended as empty string'

edgeCasesAudited.edgeCase: Stream cancelled mid-delta (turn_aborted event) clears the array; subsequent stream starts from empty
edgeCasesAudited.assertedAt: message-adapter.streaming-tool-use.parity.test.ts:E5.6c 'turn_aborted clears streamingToolUses (parity with REPL.tsx:1609 setStreamingToolUses([]) on stream cancellation)' — feeds turn_started + block_start + delta + turn_aborted and asserts streamingToolUses === [] AND isStreaming === false. E5.6a covers the turn_started boundary; E5.6b covers the per-tool slot removal on tool_call_completed.

edgeCasesAudited.edgeCase: Transcript snapshot identity changes when streamingToolUses changes (new reference) but is stable across re-derivations with the same input
edgeCasesAudited.assertedAt: message-adapter.streaming-tool-use.parity.test.ts:E5.7 (asserts new-reference half via .not.toBe across distinct sequences) + use-session-transcript.streaming-tool-use.parity.test.ts:B5.6 (asserts the useMemo wrapper that anchors stability on state.events identity)

edgeCasesAudited.edgeCase: Multi-megabyte cumulative unparsedToolInput does not regex over the whole string per delta (parity with upstream perf — handler does immutable concat only, no full-string scans)
edgeCasesAudited.assertedAt: message-adapter.streaming-tool-use.parity.test.ts:E5.8 'a single delta with a multi-kilobyte partial_json appends in O(n) ...' (256 KB cumulative, 2s bound)

hiddenCoupling.note: The slot removal on tool_call_completed matches by entry.contentBlock.id === callId. This is correct given that tool_input_block_start populates contentBlock.id from callId (line 712) when contentBlockRaw is missing/malformed, but it implicitly assumes the Anthropic adapter (R6) will emit the matching id on the contentBlock when it forwards the real Anthropic tool_use block. If a future adapter forwards a real Anthropic tool_use block whose id differs from the AgenC callId used for tool_call_completed, the slot will not be removed and the synthetic streaming cell will linger until the next turn_started or turn_aborted clear. Upstream avoids this because it filters via inProgressToolUseIDs in Messages.tsx:446. AgenC also has openTools / inProgressToolUseIDs available on the snapshot, so the filter could be moved to <Messages> if a divergence ever appears. Documented as a coupling note rather than a blocker because R6 (the Anthropic adapter forwarding row) is the row that actually nails down which id flows through.

unverifiedAssumptions.assumption: The adapter is fed unique events per call (the de-dupe via the seen set is by event key, and use-session-transcript.ts also de-dupes by key in its reducer). Two real adapter invocations with the same logical event but different keys could double-append to streamingToolUses.
unverifiedAssumptions.verified: Yes — message-adapter.ts:579-582 iterates with seen.add(event.key) gating per-call; use-session-transcript.ts:33-54 reducer de-dupes by eventKey at the hook level. Both layers are therefore key-keyed.

unverifiedAssumptions.assumption: B5.4 (retried-stream guard) only de-dupes when the SAME (index, contentBlock.id) repeats. A retried stream that re-uses the same index but with a different generated id (e.g. provider regenerates id on retry) would still create two slots, which is intentional because the upstream Messages filter keys on contentBlock.id.
unverifiedAssumptions.verified: Yes — implementation lines 720-724 and the test at line 249-258 both confirm de-dupe is keyed on (index, contentBlock.id).

summary: Re-review after R5 was extended to clear streamingToolUses on turn_aborted (parity with REPL.tsx:1609 setStreamingToolUses([]) on stream cancellation). All seven required behaviors are now implemented in runtime/src/tui/openclaude/message-adapter.ts and exposed via runtime/src/tui/openclaude/use-session-transcript.ts; all eight matrix edge cases have explicit asserting tests. Implementation verified by inspection at message-adapter.ts lines 613-622: the turn_aborted case now sets streamingToolUses.length = 0 immediately after clearing streamingText / isStreaming, with a comment citing REPL.tsx:1609. The new test E5.6c (lines 128-140) drives turn_started + block_start + delta + turn_aborted and asserts both streamingToolUses === [] and isStreaming === false, covering the matrix's updated 'Stream cancelled mid-delta (turn_aborted event)' edge case directly. No regressions: E5.6a still covers the turn_started boundary, E5.6b still covers the per-tool slot removal on tool_call_completed, and the previously-flagged hidden-coupling note about turn_aborted not clearing has been resolved by this change. By inspection, the test command 'cd runtime && npx vitest run src/tui/openclaude/message-adapter.streaming-tool-use.parity.test.ts src/tui/openclaude/use-session-transcript.streaming-tool-use.parity.test.ts' would still pass: 15 tests in the adapter parity file (E5.1, E5.2, E5.2b, E5.3, E5.4, E5.5, E5.6a, E5.6b, E5.6c, E5.7, E5.8, B5.2, B5.3, B5.4, B5.5) plus 3 in the hook parity file (B5.6 sub-tests) = 18 total. The remaining low-severity hidden-coupling note (tool_call_completed slot removal keys on contentBlock.id, coupling this row to whatever R6 forwards) is unchanged and still appropriate to revisit when R6 lands. Verdict: APPROVED.
